caseStudy.title: Subscription Revenue Recovery
caseStudy.description: Automated dunning and payment retry to reduce involuntary churn.
caseStudy.overview: A media subscription company was losing 8% of its subscriber base monthly to failed payments — most of which were recoverable card declines rather than intentional cancellations. Manual follow-up via email was ineffective and delayed.
caseStudy.problem: Failed payments were retried only once, 24 hours after the initial failure, with no smart retry logic accounting for card issuer patterns. Recovery emails were generic and sent days after the failure, when customer intent had already faded.
caseStudy.solution: An intelligent dunning system was implemented with ML-optimized retry timing based on card network patterns. Personalised recovery email sequences were triggered within minutes of payment failure, with contextual messaging based on the failure reason. In-product payment update prompts were added for mobile users.

results.summary: Involuntary churn is no longer a major revenue leak. The recovery system operates fully automatically, requiring no manual intervention, and continues to improve as it learns from payment outcomes.
